title: DevFlow project config
description: DevFlow project configuration. The LOCAL tier (the /devflow:implement, /devflow:review, /devflow:docs skills you run inside Claude Code) works with NO config file at all — every value has a sane built-in default. You only need this file if you enable the GitHub Actions automation (the optional cloud/autopilot tier). See docs/cloud-setup.md.

$schema.description: Path/URL to this schema for editor autocomplete and validation. Ignored at load time.

base_branch.description: Branch that PRs target by default. Used as the review/merge base.

claude_model.description: Claude model for AI-powered workflows (individual workflows may override).

devflow_version.description: Git ref (tag, branch, or commit SHA) of The01Geek/devflow-autopilot that the cloud-tier workflows fetch the plugin from at runtime (the vendor-plugin composite action's fetch branch — i.e. a thin install where the plugin tree is NOT committed). install.sh pins this to the commit it installed from (when no jq/node/python3 is available at install time it stays empty and you must set it by hand). Bump it to update. Ignored when the plugin is committed into .devflow/vendor/devflow/ (DEVFLOW_VENDOR=1) or when running in the source repo. Empty by default on purpose: the fetch branch fails loud on an empty ref rather than silently tracking mutable main, so an un-pinned thin install is caught instead of drifting.

providers.description: OPT-IN, BEST-EFFORT third-party model providers for the CLOUD tier (issue #313). A named map of Anthropic-compatible endpoints (OpenRouter, Z.ai, Kimi/Moonshot, LiteLLM gateways, …) that the devflow / devflow_implement / devflow_runner sections can each route to via their own `provider` key. With no providers map and no per-section `provider`, cloud behavior matches the Anthropic-OAuth default (unchanged for a given `claude_model`; the runner's default-path model now resolves from base-ref config). The provider API credential is NOT named here — it is always the fixed repository secret DEVFLOW_PROVIDER_API_KEY (kept greppable/auditable and fail-closed on absence). Anthropic does not support routing Claude Code to non-Claude models, so this is best-effort. See docs/cloud-setup.md.

base_url.description: Anthropic-compatible base URL, e.g. 'https://openrouter.ai/api' or 'https://api.z.ai/api/anthropic'. Exported to the action step as ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL.

auth.description: How DEVFLOW_PROVIDER_API_KEY is presented. 'bearer' (most gateways, incl. OpenRouter): the key rides as the anthropic_api_key action input AND as ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN (the Authorization: Bearer header) — the documented two-slot recipe. 'api_key': the key is passed as the anthropic_api_key input only (x-api-key).

timeout_ms.description: Optional request timeout in milliseconds, exported as API_TIMEOUT_MS (e.g. 3000000 for a 50-minute ceiling on slow gateway routes). Omitted → the action/client default.

effort_supported.description: Whether this provider accepts the --effort flag. Default false: many gateways reject unknown params with HTTP 400, so --effort is dropped for a provider unless this is explicitly true. The Anthropic default path keeps --effort (for a schema-valid, non-empty effort value).

env.description: Extra environment variables exported verbatim into the job environment (consumed by the action step) (e.g. ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL, CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL so background/subagent calls resolve to a real model the endpoint serves; CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_BETAS='1' to avoid anthropic-beta-header gateway 400s; CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER='0' to preserve gateway prompt caching — two distinct concerns, see docs/cloud-setup.md). See docs/cloud-setup.md for the worked OpenRouter/Z.ai maps.

devflow.description: Shared DevFlow settings. allowed_bots/allowed_users gate triggering across ALL DevFlow workflows (devflow.yml, devflow-implement.yml, devflow-review.yml, devflow-runner.yml); workpad_marker is used by /devflow:implement; effort and allowed_tools apply specifically to the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml).

provider.description: Optional: name of a `providers` entry to route the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml) through, instead of the Anthropic default. Requires the DEVFLOW_PROVIDER_API_KEY repo secret. Unset → Anthropic-OAuth default (issue #313).

claude_model.description: Optional per-section model override for the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml). Takes precedence over the global claude_model. Unset → global claude_model (issue #313).

allowed_bots.description: Bot logins allowed to trigger DevFlow workflows (comma-separated). 'claude' is Anthropic's GitHub App identity; add your own bot/automation logins (e.g. dependabot).

allowed_users.description: Human logins allowed to trigger DevFlow command/implement workflows (comma-separated), in addition to the write/admin/maintain collaborator requirement. '*' (default) allows any collaborator; narrow it to restrict to specific logins. Bots are governed separately by allowed_bots.

workpad_marker.description: Marker line identifying the persistent /devflow:implement workpad comment on issues. One comment per issue whose body starts with this marker.

effort.description: Reasoning effort for the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml). Passed explicitly via --effort on the Anthropic default path (including the default 'high'), so the effort is pinned rather than relying on the model's built-in default; a provider-routed section drops --effort unless its provider sets effort_supported: true (issue #313).

allowed_tools.description: Extra --allowed-tools entries for the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml), APPENDED on top of devflow's built-in base list. Use claude-code-action tool syntax, e.g. "Bash(make:*)", "Bash(docker compose:*)". Does not apply to /devflow:implement — see devflow_implement.allowed_tools. Empty/omitted → base list only.

execution_diagnostics_enabled.description: Gates whether the cloud-tier workflows that run claude-code-action surface the run's execution diagnostics after the claude step — the run summary (is_error, num_turns, duration_ms, total_cost_usd, permission_denials_count) and the permission-denial detail (count when available, else reported unavailable; per-denial tool_name + truncated tool_input when the execution file carries it) — to the job log and $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY via scripts/surface-execution-diagnostics.sh. A pure read-only diagnostic: it never changes the job's pass/fail result, adds no token scope, and uploads no artifact. true (the default) enables the surfacing; false is for quieter runs. Absent config resolves to true (enabled by default). Read by the 'Surface execution diagnostics' step in each cloud-tier workflow.

execution_transcript_artifact_enabled.description: Opt-in: upload the engine's token-scrubbed execution transcript as a 7-day run artifact. Default false; the scrub is a blocklist, so enable only if run artifacts are acceptable exposure for this repo's access model.

provider.description: Optional: name of a `providers` entry to route the /devflow:implement action (devflow-implement.yml) through, instead of the Anthropic default. Requires the DEVFLOW_PROVIDER_API_KEY repo secret. Unset → Anthropic-OAuth default (issue #313).

claude_model.description: Optional per-section model override for /devflow:implement (devflow-implement.yml), e.g. 'z-ai/glm-5.2'. Takes precedence over the global claude_model. Unset → global claude_model (issue #313).

implement_pr_state.description: Final state of the PR a /devflow:implement run produces. 'ready_for_review' (the default) publishes the PR in Phase 4.3 via `gh pr ready`, exactly as before. 'draft' leaves it the draft created in Phase 3.1 (the run still finalizes with Status: Complete and the outcome reaction, but does not publish, so the cloud review and CI ready_for_review listener do not auto-fire until a human publishes). Any other value — missing key, empty string, or an unrecognized string — resolves to 'ready_for_review'; default-to-publish is the safe direction. See docs/implement-skill.md for the full Phase 4.3 behavior.

allowed_tools.description: Extra --allowed-tools entries for the /devflow:implement path (devflow-implement.yml), APPENDED on top of devflow's built-in base list. The implement path does NOT inherit devflow.allowed_tools, so list every extra tool you want during implementation here. Empty/omitted → base list only.

update_branch_checkpoints.description: Master switch for the base-branch update checkpoints (issue #448). true (the default) brings the feature branch up to date with base_branch at four checkpoints of a /devflow:implement run — resume/adopt (Phase 1.4), pre-draft-PR (Phase 3.1), each fix iteration + loop exit of the review-and-fix loop under --push-each-iteration (Checkpoint 3), and pre-ready (Phase 4.3) — via scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh, so every state the merge gate or an auto-review consumes is current, including the terminal pushed state. This same key also governs the checkpoint inside a standalone /devflow:review-and-fix --push-each-iteration run. Disabled exactly when the value serializes to the string 'false' via config-get.sh — an explicit JSON false, the JSON string "false", or [false] (arrays comma-join); a missing key, empty string, or any other value leaves the checkpoints enabled (issue #312 valid-falsy discipline). A consumer repo without an up-to-date branch-protection rule keeps working unchanged apart from ordinary base merges on feature branches. See docs/implement-skill.md.

stall_backstop.description: Cloud-only workflow-level backstop for /devflow:implement (devflow-implement.yml). A headless single-shot run can end mid-lifecycle (e.g. right after `gh pr create`) yet report success, because the SDK session ends the moment the model emits a tool-call-free turn. After the `claude` step, the backstop reads the issue workpad Status (never PR draft state): a terminal Status (🎉 Complete / 👎 Blocked / 💥 Failed / 🛑 Cancelled) is a no-op, an interim Status (🚀 …) auto-resumes `/devflow:implement <n>` up to `max_resume_attempts`, and an exhausted cap or an unreadable Status fails the job loud with a distinct issue comment. Every fail-loud exit reached after reading a genuinely interim Status also flips the workpad Status to 💥 Failed (best-effort) so a dead run stops reporting itself as still working. A cancelled run (job.status=cancelled) is a decided ending, not a stall: the backstop resumes nothing, flips an interim workpad to 🛑 Cancelled (best-effort, no comment, no resume attempt consumed), and leaves terminal/unreadable/auth-failure classes untouched (issue #498). Purely additive: absent/disabled, the job behaves exactly as before.

enabled.description: Master switch for the stall backstop. true (the default) enables the post-run status check + auto-resume/fail-loud behavior; false skips the backstop entirely (no status read, no resume, no fail, no comment) so the job stays green on a mid-lifecycle stop, exactly as before this feature. An unrecognized/missing value resolves to true — the safe, honest-failure direction.

max_resume_attempts.description: Hard cap on automatic resume attempts before the backstop fails loud. 0 means detect-and-fail-loud only (no auto-resume). N means up to N auto-resumes, each announced by a distinct issue comment, before the job exits non-zero. A negative or non-integer value resolves to the default 2.

devflow_runner.description: Settings for the reusable runner that backs the automated reviewer (devflow-review.yml → devflow-runner.yml).

provider.description: Optional: name of a `providers` entry to route the automated reviewer (devflow-runner.yml) through, instead of the Anthropic default. Requires the DEVFLOW_PROVIDER_API_KEY repo secret (carried into the runner via devflow-review.yml's `secrets: inherit`). Unset → Anthropic-OAuth default, so review stays on Claude (issue #313).

claude_model.description: Optional per-section model override for the automated reviewer (devflow-runner.yml). Takes precedence over the global claude_model. Unset → global claude_model (issue #313).

provision_env.description: Opt the automated reviewer into provisioning a build environment. When true, devflow-runner.yml runs the setup-project-env action (Python/Node/PHP + service containers + `setup.install`) BEFORE Claude runs, and the read-only `review` tool profile gains the freeform build/verify tools you list in `devflow_runner.allowed_tools` (see that property) so the reviewer can actually build/test the PR instead of punting build-dependent claims; an empty/absent `allowed_tools` means no build tools are added (the runner warns). SECURITY: this flag, the `allowed_tools` list, AND the `setup` block are read from the TRUSTED base ref (your repo's base branch), never the PR head, so a PR cannot enable provisioning, grant itself tools, or inject install commands. The runner also strips a catastrophic-tier deny-list (file-mutation tools + raw-shell/eval/privilege Bash) from `allowed_tools` regardless of content. But the build tools still run the PR author's code (e.g. an npm package's postinstall) under devflow-review.yml's pull_request_target write token — enable only if you accept running untrusted PR build steps. Absent/false (the default) → the runner is unchanged: light, read-only, no build tools, no provisioning.

allowed_tools.description: Freeform build/verify tools (claude-code-action syntax, e.g. "Bash(go:*)", "Bash(cargo:*)", "Bash(npm:*)") APPENDED to the automated reviewer's read-only `review` profile — but ONLY when `provision_env: true` above. Read from the TRUSTED base ref (your repo's base branch), never the PR head, so a PR cannot grant itself tools. This makes build-aware review language-agnostic: list your toolchain here, no DevFlow release needed. A deterministic deny-list floor is enforced by devflow-runner.yml when the list is consumed — file-mutation tools (Edit, Write, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit) and raw-shell/eval/privilege Bash (bash, sh, zsh, eval, exec, source, sudo) are stripped (with a warning) regardless of what is listed here, so the catastrophic tier can never reach a write-token job reviewing untrusted code. When `provision_env` is false the reviewer is byte-for-byte read-only regardless of this list. /devflow:init auto-populates this per detected language and enriches it from your repo's real build setup.

devflow_review_and_fix.description: Settings for /devflow:review-and-fix: the fix-loop iteration cap, the fix-severity routing threshold, and per-run subagent effectiveness telemetry. See docs/shadow-review.md and docs/efficiency-trace.md.

fix_severity_threshold.description: The /devflow:review-and-fix fix loop routes to the fixer every finding whose severity is at or above this threshold (severity ordering: critical > important > suggestion); findings below it stay advisory. At the default 'important', the loop fixes Critical + Important/Major and parks Suggestion/Minor as advisory — identical to the historical hard-coded behavior. Set 'suggestion' for a more aggressive fixer (Suggestion/Minor also routed to the fixer, bounded by max_iterations and the convergence check) or 'critical' for a conservative one. Independent of devflow_review.verdict_severity_threshold, except that every REJECT-driving finding is always fixable regardless of this key. A missing/wrong-type/unknown value falls back to the default with a stderr breadcrumb (never aborts a run).

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of fix-loop iterations /devflow:review-and-fix runs before exiting with its latest verdict. Higher converges on a clean verdict more often at greater subagent cost; lower saves cost. A value below 1 is clamped up to 1, and a non-integer/empty/unparseable value (or an unreadable config) falls back to the default. No upper bound is imposed. See docs/shadow-review.md.

efficiency_telemetry_enabled.description: When true, /devflow:review-and-fix derives a per-subagent effectiveness trace at Loop Exit, prints it to chat, and persists one per-run record to .devflow/logs/efficiency/. When false, the loop neither renders the trace nor writes any file under .devflow/logs/. Derivation is best-effort: a failure never aborts the fix loop.

efficiency_cut_candidate_min_dispatch.description: Minimum number of times a Phase-3 subagent must have been dispatched (across runs) before an all-null/noise agent is flagged as a cut candidate. Defined here so the config surface is stable; consumed by the follow-up cross-run analyzer, not by /devflow:review-and-fix itself. The per-run record carries this value forward for that analyzer.

devflow_review.description: Settings for the standalone /devflow:review engine. See docs/workflow-triggers.md (live progress comment) and docs/efficiency-trace.md (telemetry + effectiveness trace).

verdict_severity_threshold.description: Moves ONLY the REJECT line of the shared review engine's Phase 4.2 verdict computation: a finding at or above this severity (severity ordering: critical > important > suggestion), excluding deferral-demoted ones, drives REJECT. All other Phase 4.2 rules and verdict labels are untouched. At the default 'critical' (or when the key is absent) verdict computation is byte-identical to the historical behavior. Both standalone /devflow:review and the Phase-3 pass of /devflow:review-and-fix inherit this. A missing/wrong-type/unknown value falls back to the default with a stderr breadcrumb (never aborts a run).

live_progress_comment_enabled.description: When true, a PR-mode /devflow:review authors a live progress comment for that run (a run-keyed <!-- devflow:review-progress run=<id>-<attempt> --> comment — one per review run, so a later run posts its own rather than overwriting an earlier run's) incrementally as it works — a blueprint of the phases up front, then per-phase updates (diff classification, checklist results, each Phase-3 agent's findings as they land, the verdict), finalizing with the run-telemetry summary and effectiveness trace. When false, /devflow:review falls back to producing its report once at the end (no incremental updates). Independent of devflow_review_and_fix.efficiency_telemetry_enabled, which separately gates the embedded telemetry/trace.

stale_prose.description: Gates the shared review engine's Phase 0.6 deterministic stale-counted-prose lint (scripts/stale-prose-lint.py, issue #423), which flags diff-added prose asserting counts/ranges/sums/deny-absolutes that the same PR's later commits outgrow or falsify. Inherited identically by standalone /devflow:review, the Phase-3 pass of /devflow:review-and-fix, and the shadow pass, since all three drive the same engine. Purely additive: absent, the phase runs with its defaults (enabled, findings at 'important').

enabled.description: Master switch for Phase 0.6. true (the default) runs the lint and enters each STALE row as an engine finding; false disables the phase with a recorded config-disabled note (never a silent skip). Any value other than an explicit false resolves to enabled — the safe, feature-on-by-default direction.

severity.description: The engine-finding severity assigned to each STALE row from Phase 0.6, which then participates in Phase 4.2 verdict computation exactly like any other finding at that severity. UNRESOLVABLE rows are informational notes and never gate regardless of this value. A missing/wrong-type/unknown/empty value falls back to the default 'important' with a stderr breadcrumb (never aborts a run).

require_up_to_date.description: When true (the default), the devflow-review auto-trigger defers — posting a neutral 'waiting: branch behind base' Devflow Review check instead of running the engine — while the PR branch is behind its configured base_branch, so an LLM review that a forthcoming branch update would invalidate is not wasted. The review auto-re-triggers on the next reviewable event (synchronize, or other-CI completion). A neutral required check does NOT itself block merge: this precondition is a cost optimization, not a correctness gate — pair it with branch protection's 'require branches up to date' if staleness must block. When false, restores the unconditional auto-trigger for this precondition. Evaluated by scripts/derive-review-preconditions.sh; an unverifiable comparison fails closed (defer, recoverable via a later event or the check's Re-run button).

require_ci_green.description: When true (the default), the devflow-review auto-trigger defers — posting a neutral 'waiting: other CI not green' Devflow Review check instead of running the engine — until every other signal on the PR head (GitHub Actions workflow runs excluding the Devflow Review workflow itself, legacy commit statuses, and non-Actions check runs) has completed without failing (success, skipped, and neutral conclusions all count as green, so a path-filtered or optional workflow never wedges the review), so a code-quality verdict is never produced for known-broken code. Generic: no CI job names are referenced. A head with NO other CI at all is treated as satisfied (nothing to wait on), so a CI-less repo is reviewed immediately and never wedged. The review auto-re-triggers when other CI completes — a workflow_run/check_suite completed event, or (since issue #310) a legacy commit-status `status` event with `state == 'success'` for repos whose CI reports only via the commit-status API (classic Jenkins, legacy CircleCI); the workflow_run trigger's `workflows:` list must name the repo's CI workflows, a GitHub platform requirement. When false, restores the unconditional auto-trigger for this precondition. Evaluated by scripts/derive-review-preconditions.sh; an unverifiable query fails closed (defer, recoverable via a later event or the check's Re-run button).

stall_backstop.description: Cloud-only workflow-level backstop for the review engine. A headless review run can end `success` with NO verdict — the SDK session ends the moment the model emits a tool-call-free turn (e.g. after ScheduleWakeup while a Phase-3 agent is still pending), so the required `Devflow Review` check fails 'incomplete — re-run needed' and a human must manually re-trigger. On the AUTO-review path (devflow-review.yml's finalize_check), which derives the verdict for the head, the backstop fires when the run concludes incomplete with no verdict positively observed for the head — exactly this early-quit — and posts a `/devflow:review` re-trigger comment (authored by a freshly minted App token) up to `max_resume_attempts` per head, so the review re-runs without human intervention. The MANUAL /devflow:review comment path (devflow.yml) has no verdict-derivation step, so it fires the same backstop only on the #356 dead-run signal (the engine ending is_error, or the claude step ending failure — a hard death), not on a clean success-with-no-verdict; that manual-path case is covered only by the headless-wait skill/grounding prose. When the cap is exhausted, or no workflow-capable App token is configured (DEVFLOW_APP_ID unset — a GITHUB_TOKEN-authored comment never re-triggers a workflow), behavior degrades to exactly the pre-existing dead-end flip, with a breadcrumb naming why the backstop did not fire. Because the re-trigger re-enters the SAME manual /devflow:review comment gate, auto-resume additionally requires the minting App's bot login to be in `devflow.allowed_bots` (the same prerequisite the implement backstop carries); otherwise the comment posts but the gate declines it and no review re-runs. This is the review-side sibling of devflow_implement.stall_backstop. A cancelled run is excluded from auto-resume on every DevFlow backstop (a cancel is a decided ending, not a benign timing stall — issue #498); the review tier's exclusion is already correct at source (the dead-run signal fires only on is_error/failure, never on cancellation). Purely additive: absent/disabled, the review behaves exactly as before.

enabled.description: Master switch for the review stall backstop. true (the default) enables the no-verdict auto-resume; false skips it entirely (no resume comment) so an incomplete review degrades to the dead-end flip exactly as before this feature. An unrecognized/missing value resolves to true — the safe, honest-failure direction.

max_resume_attempts.description: Hard cap on automatic review-resume attempts per head before the backstop stops firing and the run degrades to the dead-end flip. 0 disables auto-resume (detect-and-flip only). N means up to N auto-resumes per head, each carrying a distinct `<!-- devflow:review-backstop head=<sha> attempt=<n> -->` marker comment that the count is derived from. A negative or non-integer value resolves to the default 2.

agent_overrides.description: Per-subagent model/effort overrides for the shared /devflow:review engine. Applies to BOTH standalone /devflow:review AND the Phase-3 review pass of /devflow:review-and-fix (and thus /devflow:implement), since both drive the same engine. Keys are the nine review-engine subagent identifiers (byte-identical to the telemetry strings) plus an optional `default` fallback. Each value optionally sets `model` (free-form model id, forwarded to the dispatch as given), `effort` (one of low/medium/high/xhigh/max — an out-of-enum value warns and falls back to the session effort rather than aborting), and/or `iterations` (issue #425; only valid value `first-only` — an agent whose resolved override carries it is excluded from the Phase-3 review roster on fix-loop iterations >= 2, enforced engine-side; an out-of-enum value warns and is dropped rather than aborting; key absent = today's behavior). Resolution is entry-level: a subagent with its own entry uses only that entry (the `default` does NOT backfill its missing fields); `default` supplies model/effort only for subagents with no entry of their own. A subagent with neither its own entry nor a `default` is dispatched exactly as today — global claude_model + session effort — with no --agents override emitted for it. See docs/review-agent-overrides.md.

devflow:checklist-deduper.description: Pins Claude Sonnet 4.6 for the dedup pass with effort 'medium' (Sonnet 4.6's recommended default; effort must be set explicitly on Sonnet 4.6 to avoid unexpected latency). NOTE: Claude Haiku rejects the `effort` parameter with HTTP 400 (effort is supported only on Opus 4.5–4.8 / Sonnet 4.6), so if you re-pin a Haiku id here the entry must NOT carry an `effort` key; scaffold-config.sh strips a stale `effort` from any Haiku-pinned override on re-scaffold.

receiving_review.description: Settings for a direct invocation of /devflow:receiving-code-review (the fix-loop principles skill). Consulted only when the skill is invoked directly; the /devflow:review-and-fix loop governs its own routing via devflow_review_and_fix.fix_severity_threshold and does NOT consult this section.

fix_severity_threshold.description: When /devflow:receiving-code-review is invoked directly and the verdict is already non-blocking, the diff is re-opened for a finding at or above this severity (severity ordering: critical > important > suggestion). At the default 'critical' this matches the historical behavior (re-open only for a Critical/blocking finding). The demonstrable-correctness-defect / documented-falsehood carve-out re-opens the diff at every threshold value — it is a correctness principle, not a severity grade. A missing/wrong-type/unknown value falls back to the default with a stderr breadcrumb (never aborts a run).

setup.description: Cloud-tier runtime provisioning: how the GitHub Actions runner is prepared BEFORE Claude runs — for the /devflow:* command path, /devflow:implement, AND the automated reviewer. /devflow:init auto-populates node_version + install per detected language (deterministic presets), then the LLM enriches php_version/extensions and services by reading docker-compose/CI/composer.json. Provisioning order: Python → Node → PHP → service containers → install lines.

python_version.description: setup-python runs ONLY if non-empty. Config (JSON) is read by a python3 resolver (config-get.sh) using the stdlib json module — no PyYAML needed for config. match-deferrals.py additionally needs Python >= 3.11 with PyYAML to parse the YAML deferred-findings blocks in PR bodies, and it runs in the cloud workflows, so keep this set (e.g. '3.11'). (workpad.py is stdlib-only — it needs Python >= 3.11 but not PyYAML.)

node_version.description: setup-node runs ONLY if non-empty. Set it (e.g. '20') if your project's tests/build need Node. When set, npm/yarn/pnpm download caching is enabled automatically IF a lockfile (package-lock.json / yarn.lock / pnpm-lock.yaml / npm-shrinkwrap.json) is present in the directory named by node_working_directory (the repo root by default).

node_working_directory.description: Directory (relative to the repo root) that holds the Node project's package.json/lockfile, for repos whose JS build lives in a subdirectory (e.g. 'jsx', 'resources/js', 'frontend') rather than at the root. When set, setup-node's download caching resolves the lockfile under this directory and /devflow:init scopes the generated Node install line into it. Empty/absent (the default) → caching and detection key off the repo root, byte-for-byte identical to the root-based behavior. Note: this only affects the auto-detected Node install line and cache resolution; setup.install lines still run from the repo root.

php_version.description: shivammathur/setup-php runs ONLY if non-empty (e.g. '8.3'). Sets the PHP runtime + Composer. Populated by /devflow:init from composer.json's require.php constraint when detectable.

php_extensions.description: CSV list of PHP extensions to install with setup-php (e.g. 'mbstring, pdo_mysql, redis, intl'). Only used when php_version is set. Populated by /devflow:init from composer.json require.ext-* and the project's service needs.

php_tools.description: CSV list of PHP tools for setup-php (e.g. 'composer:v2, phpunit'). Optional — Composer is set up by default. Only used when php_version is set.

services.description: Service containers (databases, caches, queues) started via `docker run -d` BEFORE the install lines, reachable from the runner on 127.0.0.1:<host-port>. NOT GitHub Actions `services:` (which can't be defined in a composite action or driven by config). Populated by /devflow:init from docker-compose.yml / .env / existing CI. The runner needs Docker (preinstalled on ubuntu-latest) and the `docker` preset's allowlist.

ports.description: Port mappings 'host:container', e.g. ['3306:3306']. The app's test config should connect to 127.0.0.1:<host-port>.

env.description: Environment variables passed as -e KEY=VALUE, e.g. {"MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD": "root", "MYSQL_DATABASE": "app_test"}.

options.description: Extra `docker run` options, one complete arg per array element (NOT a single space-separated string — that would word-split a health command's spaces). Use a health check so readiness is awaited, e.g. ["--health-cmd=mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1", "--health-interval=5s", "--health-timeout=5s", "--health-retries=20"]. Maintainer-controlled, read from the trusted base config.

install.description: Shell lines run verbatim (joined with newlines) after the language setups and service-container start. Put devflow's own deps first, then your project's (e.g. 'composer install --no-interaction'). Empty array → skipped.

claude_code_executable.description: Optional path (typically absolute) to a pre-installed Claude Code executable, passed to anthropics/claude-code-action's `path_to_claude_code_executable` input. Set it on a self-hosted runner whose OS the action's bundled installer cannot serve — notably a self-hosted WINDOWS runner (the installer is Unix-only), where you pre-install the CLI (e.g. `irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex`) and point this at the resulting `claude.exe`. When set, the action skips installation and uses this executable; when unset or empty (the default, and every Linux consumer) the input resolves to an empty string and the action's automatic-install path runs unchanged. Only a single-line, non-blank string is accepted: a non-string leaf (including a valid-falsy `false`), a non-object `setup` block, a string with an embedded newline/CR, and a whitespace-only string are each rejected and resolve to empty (auto-install), with a workflow `::warning::` naming the key so a mistyped path is not silently ignored (an absent key, a JSON `null`, and an explicit empty string are deliberate unsets and warn nothing). Read from config at TRIGGER time (the config/base-ref jobs), so its effect is post-merge-only — a PR that adds the key cannot exercise it in that PR's own run.

git_dir_pin.description: Opt in to exporting `GIT_DIR=<workspace>/.git` into the cloud job environment before the `Run Claude Code` step (issue #645). Default FALSE, and the default is the configuration that works everywhere. Enable it only on a self-hosted WINDOWS runner, where claude-code-action's `configureGitAuth` startup otherwise fails `fatal: not in a git directory` (exit 128) because it runs `git config` in the inherited working directory (issue #602). COSTS, all of which you accept by enabling it: `$GITHUB_ENV` is job-scoped and offers no removal verb, so the variable is in force for `Run Claude Code` AND every step after it, including the agent's own git operations; and any DevFlow helper that runs from a NON-ROOT working directory then resolves a `.devflow/` that does not exist, because the issue #295 repo-root readers (`config-get.sh`, `workpad.py`, `load-prompt-extension.sh`, `match-deferrals.py`, `match-lint-adjudications.py`) anchor on `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, which under ambient GIT_DIR returns the subdirectory rather than the repository root. That failure is a SILENT MISS rather than an error, so a run with this key enabled is not a config-faithful run; the helper emits a `::warning::`-grade stderr breadcrumb on every run that exports the variable. NOT honored on the implement tier, which stages and pushes commits (ambient GIT_DIR makes a stage issued from a non-root working directory record deletions across the rest of the tree); that tier records that it ignored the key. The `configureGitAuth` benefit is INFERRED from the pinned action's upstream source and a local `git config` proxy — it has not been observed on a self-hosted Windows runner. Read at TRIGGER time from a trusted tree (the `config` job's default-branch checkout; on the review tier the base-ref config `baseprovision` materializes), so its effect is post-merge-only and a value present only in a PR-head checkout has no effect.
git_dir_pin.$comment: issue #645 — decoupled from git_work_tree_pin; both default off.

git_work_tree_pin.description: Opt in to exporting `GIT_WORK_TREE=<workspace>` into the cloud job environment before the `Run Claude Code` step (issue #645). Default FALSE, and the default is the configuration that works everywhere. COST — read this before enabling: `GIT_WORK_TREE` also reaches the Claude Code CLI subprocess that installs plugins, where it makes `git clone` refuse an existing working tree, so a run that installs a REMOTE plugin marketplace dies at plugin install with `fatal: working tree '<path>' already exists.` before the agent does any work, producing no verdict at all — the outage this key was decoupled to end. Enable it ONLY if your composed marketplace list is LOCAL-ONLY, so no remote clone is ever performed; for that narrower population it fixes `configureGitAuth` while keeping working-tree resolution correct, which is the one combination that avoids the `git_dir_pin` relocation hazard entirely. Enabling it outside that population reproduces the outage. Like `git_dir_pin`, the export is job-scoped and therefore in force for `Run Claude Code` and every step after it. Read at TRIGGER time from a trusted tree, so its effect is post-merge-only and a value present only in a PR-head checkout has no effect.
git_work_tree_pin.$comment: issue #645 — decoupled from git_dir_pin; both default off.

docs.description: Documentation paths used by the /devflow:docs suite.

internal_enabled.description: When false, the /devflow:docs combined pass (and /devflow:implement's Phase 4.1 docs step) SKIPS internal-doc updates (Step 1, docs-sync-internal). Use it for repos with no internal docs tree, or to keep the implement pass from touching internal docs. Default true (run internal docs). Does not affect invoking /devflow:docs-sync-internal directly.

external_enabled.description: When false, the /devflow:docs combined pass (and /devflow:implement's Phase 4.1 docs step) SKIPS external-doc alignment (Step 2, docs-sync-external). Use it for repos with no separate customer-facing docs tree (e.g. a single flat docs/ directory). Default true (run external docs). Does not affect invoking /devflow:docs-sync-external directly.

changelog_file.description: Repository CHANGELOG file reconciled by /devflow:docs-release-notes Step 4b. The step reads the `## [version]` entry for the version in `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (a `chore: bump version` commit only confirms a bump occurred; the version is never taken from its subject) and corrects any factual claims made stale by post-commit review corrections.

labels.description: Comma-separated list of labels applied by /devflow:implement once its docs pass has run. Each entry is trimmed and empty entries are dropped, so "DevFlow, Shipped" applies both DevFlow and Shipped.

deferred.description: Settings for the follow-up issues /devflow:implement files for deferred work.

labels.description: Comma-separated list of labels applied to the follow-up issues /devflow:implement files for deferred work — both Phase 4.0 deferred-acceptance-criteria issues and Phase 4.0.5 deferred-review-finding issues. Each entry is trimmed and blank entries are dropped, so "DevFlow, Deferred" applies both DevFlow and Deferred, and a whitespace-only or all-separators value (e.g. " , ") normalizes to none, applying no labels. (A literal empty string resolves to this default rather than meaning no-labels, matching how config defaults resolve; set a whitespace value to apply none.) Labels are ensured to exist and applied best-effort after the issue is created, so a label hiccup never blocks filing. This is separate from the hardcoded `DevFlow` provenance label that retrospective detection (lib/scan.sh, lib/classify-pr-kind.jq) matches literally — only deferred-issue labeling is configurable here.

telemetry.description: Settings for the durable observability-telemetry store (issue #441). DevFlow persists every writable run's effectiveness records and durable review workpads to a dedicated long-lived orphan branch that shares no history with the base branch and is never merged into it, written via git plumbing without touching the current branch, HEAD, or the tracked working tree (it stages under gitignored .devflow/tmp/, which it removes afterwards), then pushed. The weekly retrospective reads that branch (unioned with any legacy tracked .devflow/logs/).

branch.description: Name of the long-lived orphan branch DevFlow persists observability artifacts to. Auto-created on first use if absent; verified to be a telemetry store (its tip tree holds only .devflow/logs/-shaped paths) before appending, so DevFlow never commits onto a same-named branch a consumer uses for something else. A literal empty string resolves to this default (a string key — an empty branch name is never a valid selection). Keep every workflow `push:` trigger branch-filtered so a telemetry push runs no CI (DevFlow's own workflows filter `push:` to `main`; a consumer whose `on: push` is unfiltered should add a `branches-ignore` entry for this branch).

verification_flight.description: Versioned verification-profile contract for the single-flight verification coordinator (issue #528, Wave 2). scripts/verification-flight.py is a NON-executing coordination ledger: it never launches the verification command and accepts no executable argv. Each profile DECLARES the command's identity and hermetic inputs; the helper canonicalizes {profile_version, argv, cwd, environment, toolchain, dependencies} as sorted-key compact UTF-8 JSON and derives the SHA-256 command descriptor itself (a caller cannot supply a static digest). A profile is reusable only when it is fully declared and `external_services` is `none`; an undeclared, secret-dependent, or external-service-dependent profile is non-reusable and never satisfies verification. State lives under .devflow/tmp/verification-flights/, is published atomically, and is durable only within the current checkout.

enabled.description: When true, Implement and inline Review-and-Fix coordinate each same-checkout full-verification flight through scripts/verification-flight.py (claim/mark-running/finish, attach/wait/consume) instead of relaunching the command directly. When false, callers launch the verification command directly as before. Off never weakens a guarantee: a missing, partial, timed-out, unreadable, or stale handle always falls back to a direct launch and never becomes a pass.

lease_seconds.description: Bounded owner-token lease on a `claimed` handle. An attacher waits while the lease is valid; a lease that expires before the owner records `mark-running` transitions the handle to `incomplete` (owner loss at the claim boundary), which blocks reuse and never authorizes an automatic relaunch. The helper's runtime default is the same 900s (DEFAULT_LEASE_SECONDS); callers may override per invocation via --lease-seconds. DECLARATIVE in Wave 2: the Implement / Review-and-Fix skill prose resolves only `.verification_flight.enabled` and does not yet thread this scalar into its `claim` call, so editing this key alone has no effect on a coordinated flight today — it documents the intended default and forward-compatible override surface.

wait_timeout_seconds.description: Default bound for an attacher's `wait` poll. A wait-bound expiry returns a non-mutating `wait_expired` observation and leaves an active flight unchanged; only the owner records a terminal `timed_out` after its command invocation reports a real timeout. DECLARATIVE in Wave 2: the skill prose passes `wait`'s bound via its own `--timeout`, and does not yet thread this config scalar, so editing this key alone has no effect on a coordinated flight today — like `lease_seconds` it documents the intended default and forward-compatible override surface.

profiles.description: Named verification profiles. Each profile is a data-only declaration of one verification command's identity and hermetic inputs. This namespace is DECLARATIVE ONLY in Wave 2: the helper takes its profile inline via `--input-file` and implements no lookup-by-name, so no profile name here is resolved by scripts/verification-flight.py today. The `required` list below mirrors the helper's own `_PROFILE_REQUIRED` tuple exactly, so a schema-valid profile is also declaration-valid at runtime.

profile_version.description: Monotonic version of this profile. A byte-distinct profile_version produces a distinct command descriptor and thus a distinct binding, so bumping it invalidates reuse of any prior flight.

argv.description: The exact argv of the already-authorized verification command, as a data descriptor operand. The helper NEVER executes this argv; the existing caller retains command ownership and allowlist enforcement. The leading token must be the same allowlisted literal head the caller invokes (e.g. `lib/test/run.sh`).

cwd.description: Working directory the command runs in, as a descriptor operand.

environment.description: Declared NONSECRET environment inputs that affect the command's output. Secret-dependent inputs must not appear here; a secret-dependent profile is non-reusable.

toolchain.description: Toolchain identities (interpreter/tool -> version or path identity) the command's result depends on.

dependencies.description: Dependency identities (dependency -> resolved identity) the command's result depends on. A relevant dependency change invalidates reuse.

output_roots.description: Roots the suite WRITES to. Generated verification outputs under these roots do not become inputs merely because the suite created them, so they never churn the flight key.

external_services.description: Must be `none`. A profile that depends on any external service is non-reusable and is rejected by the helper as non-hermetic.

devflow_retrospective.description: DevFlow retrospective / self-improvement loop, consumed by the /devflow:retrospective-weekly orchestrator (a locally-run skill, not a GitHub Actions workflow).

retrospective_model.description: Optional --model override for the retrospective subagent.

audit_model.description: Optional --model override for the audit subagent.

implementation_branch_prefix.description: Branch prefix your implementation bot uses for its PRs. The loop processes a PR matching this prefix, or any PR carrying the DevFlow label or closing an issue.

min_occurrences.description: Repetition gate. status == regressed always bypasses this.

cooldown_days.description: Skip re-filing a pattern's retrospective issue if its youngest open filed issue is younger than this (createdAt-based).

max_prs_per_run.description: Soft cap on PRs processed per scheduled retrospective run.

diff_byte_cap.description: Per-PR diff size ceiling (bytes) for the retrospective context bundle (lib/fetch-pr-context.sh). A PR whose embedded diff exceeds this — or whose diff can't be fetched — gets diff: null / diff_truncated: true and the analyst works from changed_files, reviews, and the issue instead. Generated/vendored file bodies (lockfiles, bundles, node_modules/vendor/dist/build) are elided before the cap is applied.

watched_authors.description: Authors whose merged PRs the loop retrospects. Defaults to devflow.allowed_bots if unset.

devflow.description: Enable the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml) and /devflow:implement (devflow-implement.yml).
